expectations.text: simplify-step-present: Output shows /simplify step in the pipeline
expectations.evidence: No /simplify step in the pipeline. Workflow definition shows: '1. system-analyzer -> 2. developer -> 3. qa -> 4. qa + code-reviewer (PARALLEL)'. The /simplify skill is never invoked.

expectations.text: remediation-loop-shown: Output shows a remediation loop when blocking findings occur
expectations.evidence: When Code Reviewer finds 2 Critical findings, an ad-hoc remediation step is created (Step 4.5 Developer fixes -> Step 4.6 Code Reviewer re-reviews -> Approved). However, labeled as 'Ad-hoc' and the output notes the skill has no defined protocol for this.

expectations.text: remediation-max-2-cycles: Output mentions max 2 remediation cycles before escalation
expectations.evidence: No mention of max 2 remediation cycles. The output explicitly identifies this as a gap in the 'Observations on Skill Gaps' table: 'Max retry count for re-review loop -- No limit on how many fix-and-re-review cycles are allowed -- Could loop indefinitely if Developer keeps introducing issues.'
States 'Selected Workflow: Bug Fix' with reasoning: 'อาการที่ user เห็นคือ 500 error (bug) แม้ root cause จะเป็น performance แต่ workflow ที่เหมาะสมคือ Bug Fix'.

expectations.text: system-analyzer-first: System Analyzer runs before Developer
expectations.evidence: Step 1 is 'System Analyzer -- Diagnose Root Cause' and Step 2 is 'Developer -- Implement Fix'. Workflow definition confirms: '1. system-analyzer -> diagnose root cause, 2. developer -> implement fix'.

expectations.text: root-cause-passed-to-developer: System Analyzer findings passed to Developer with specifics
expectations.evidence: Developer's prompt includes 'Root cause from System Analyzer' with specific details: repository FindByCitizenID has no LIMIT, N+1 pattern with consent_details, handler context timeout 5s, and file references (consent_repository.go:87, :95, get_consent.go:34, consent_handler.go:52).
